irish: Níl aon tinteán mar do thinteán féin
english: There's no hearth like your own hearth

irish: Is fearr rith maith ná drochsheasamh
english: A good run is better than a bad stand

irish: Mol an óige agus tiocfaidh sí
english: Praise the youth and they will flourish

irish: Is minic a bhris béal duine a shúil
english: Often a person’s mouth breaks their eye

irish: Tús maith leath na hoibre
english: A good start is half the work

irish: Ní hé lá na gaoithe lá na scolb
english: The windy day is not the day of the roof tiles

irish: An té a bhíonn siúlach, bíonn scéalach
english: He who travels has stories to tell

irish: Moladh an té nach bhfuil ina sheasamh sa bhóthar
english: Praise the one who doesn’t stand in the road

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte
english: Health is better than wealth

irish: Ní bhíonn saoi gan locht
english: No wise man is without fault

irish: Is fearr Gaeilge briste ná Béarla cliste
english: Broken Irish is better than clever English

irish: An rud is annamh is iontach
english: What is rare is wonderful

irish: Is maith an scéalaí an aimsir
english: Time is a great storyteller

irish: Ní bhíonn in aon rud ach seal
english: Nothing lasts forever

irish: Is fearr cara sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán
english: A friend in court is better than a pound in the purse

irish: Ní hé méid an bháid ach an méid den chriú
english: It's not the size of the boat but the crew

irish: An té nach gcuireann san earrach ní bhaineann sé san fhómhar
english: He who doesn't sow in spring won't reap in autumn

irish: Níor bhris focal maith fiacail riamh
english: A kind word never broke a tooth

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras
english: Hunger is a good sauce

irish: Is fearr mall ná go brách
english: Better late than never

irish: An té a bhíonn láidir ní foláir dó bheith glic
english: He who is strong must be clever

irish: Bíonn blas ar an mbeagán
english: There is taste in a little

irish: Ní hé lá na báistí lá na bpáistí
english: A rainy day is not the day for children

irish: Is minic a chuala mé seanfhocal ach ní fhaca mé é
english: I have often heard a proverb but never saw it

irish: An té a bhíonn foighneach bíonn bua aige
english: He who is patient succeeds

irish: Is fearr an tslí bheag ná an tslí mhór nach mbíonn ann
english: The small road is better than the big road that isn't there

irish: Bíonn súil le muir ach ní bhíonn súil le tír
english: One expects the sea but not the land

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an fhírinne searbh
english: The truth is often bitter

irish: Ní thagann ciall roimh aois
english: Wisdom does not come before age

irish: Is fearr foghlaim mall ná gan foghlaim ar chor ar bith
english: Better to learn slowly than not at all

irish: An té a luíonn le madaí éireoidh sé le dreancaidí
english: He who lies with dogs rises with fleas

irish: Níor tháinig aon mhaith riamh as an leisce
english: No good ever came from laziness

irish: Bíonn an fhírinne searbh
english: The truth can be bitter

irish: Is maith an cara an t-am
english: Time is a good friend

irish: Ní bhíonn bua gan iarracht
english: There is no victory without effort

irish: Is maith an rud an fhoighne
english: Patience is a good thing

irish: Ní bhíonn dhá lá mar a chéile
english: No two days are the same

irish: Bíonn gach rud deacair sula mbíonn sé éasca
english: Everything is difficult before it is easy

irish: Ní bhíonn grá gan locht
english: There is no love without flaws

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an ceart lag
english: The right is often weak

irish: An té a bhíonn saibhir bíonn cairde aige
english: The rich man has many friends

irish: Is fearr obair ná caint
english: Work is better than talk

irish: Ní bhíonn ciall cheannaigh níos fearr ná ciall foghlamtha
english: Bought wisdom is no better than learned wisdom

irish: Is maith an fear an t-anlann
english: Good company makes the meal

irish: Bíonn an saol gearr
english: Life is short

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an bua ag an té foighneach
english: The patient person often wins

irish: Ní bhíonn cairde gan locht
english: Friends are not without faults

irish: Is fearr an t-eolas ná an t-airgead
english: Knowledge is better than money

irish: Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an smacht
english: Success depends on discipline

irish: Is maith an rud an cairdeas
english: Friendship is a good thing
